<source_slice path="README.md" lines="147-177">
147: ## Testing
148: 
149: Most of the unit tests in Logstash are written using [rspec](http://rspec.info/) for the Ruby parts. For the Java parts, we use [junit](https://junit.org). For testing you can use the *test* `rake` tasks and the `bin/rspec` command, see instructions below:
150: 
151: ### Core tests
152: 
153: 1- To run the core tests you can use the Gradle task:
154: 
155:     ./gradlew test
156: 
157:   or use the `rspec` tool to run all tests or run a specific test:
158: 
159:     bin/rspec
160:     bin/rspec spec/foo/bar_spec.rb
161: 
162:   Note that before running the `rspec` command for the first time you need to set up the RSpec test dependencies by running:
163: 
164:     ./gradlew bootstrap
165: 
166: 2- To run the subset of tests covering the Java codebase only run:
167: 
168:     ./gradlew javaTests
169: 
170: 3- To execute the complete test-suite including the integration tests run:
171: 
172:     ./gradlew check
173: 
174: 4- To execute a single Ruby test run:
175: 
176:     SPEC_OPTS="-fd -P logstash-core/spec/logstash/api/commands/default_metadata_spec.rb" ./gradlew :logstash-core:rubyTests --tests org.logstash.RSpecTests
177: 
</source_slice>

### Existing BufferedTokenizer specs

`logstash-core/spec/logstash/util/buftok_spec.rb`
```ruby
18: require "spec_helper"
19: 
20: describe  FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer  do
21:   subject { FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer.new }
22: 
23:   it "should tokenize a single token" do
24:     expect(subject.extract("foo\n")).to eq(["foo"])
25:   end
26: 
27:   it "should merge multiple token" do
28:     expect(subject.extract("foo")).to eq([])
29:     expect(subject.extract("bar\n")).to eq(["foobar"])
30:   end
31: 
32:   it "should tokenize multiple token" do
33:     expect(subject.extract("foo\nbar\n")).to eq(["foo", "bar"])
34:   end
35: 
36:   it "should ignore empty payload" do
37:     expect(subject.extract("")).to eq([])
38:     expect(subject.extract("foo\nbar")).to eq(["foo"])
39:   end
40: 
41:   it "should tokenize empty payload with newline" do
42:     expect(subject.extract("\n")).to eq([""])
43:     expect(subject.extract("\n\n\n")).to eq(["", "", ""])
44:   end
45: 
46:   describe 'flush' do
47:     let(:data) { "content without a delimiter" }
48:     before(:each) do
49:       subject.extract(data)
50:     end
51: 
52:     it "emits the contents of the buffer" do
53:       expect(subject.flush).to eq(data)
54:     end
55: 
56:     it "resets the state of the buffer" do
57:       subject.flush
58:       expect(subject).to be_empty
59:     end
60:

63: 
64:       it "validates size limit" do

66:         expect { FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer.new("\n", 0) }.to raise_error(java.lang.IllegalArgumentException, "Size limit must be positive")
67:       end

52:     @JRubyMethod(name = "initialize", optional = 2)
53:     public IRubyObject init(final ThreadContext context, IRubyObject[] args) {
54:         if (args.length >= 1) {
55:             this.delimiter = args[0].convertToString();
56:         }
57:         if (args.length == 2) {
58:             final int sizeLimit = args[1].convertToInteger().getIntValue();
59:             if (sizeLimit <= 0) {
60:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException("Size limit must be positive");
61:             }
62:             this.sizeLimit = sizeLimit;
63:             this.hasSizeLimit = true;

65:         this.inputSize = 0;
66:         return this;

68: 
69:     /**
70:      * Extract takes an arbitrary string of input data and returns an array of
71:      * tokenized entities, provided there were any available to extract.  This
72:      * makes for easy processing of datagrams using a pattern like:
73:      *
74:      * {@code tokenizer.extract(data).map { |entity| Decode(entity) }.each do}
75:      *
76:      * @param context ThreadContext
77:      * @param data    IRubyObject
78:      * @return Extracted tokens
79:      */
80:     @JRubyMethod
81:     @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes")
82:     public RubyArray extract(final ThreadContext context, IRubyObject data) {
83:         final RubyArray entities = data.convertToString().split(delimiter, -1);
84:         if (hasSizeLimit) {
85:             final int entitiesSize = ((RubyString) entities.first()).size();
86:             if (inputSize + entitiesSize > sizeLimit) {
87:                 throw new IllegalStateException("input buffer full");
88:             }
89:             this.inputSize = inputSize + entitiesSize;
90:         }
91:         input.append(entities.shift(context));
92:         if (entities.isEmpty()) {
93:             return RubyUtil.RUBY.newArray();
94:         }

102:     /**
103:      * Flush the contents of the input buffer, i.e. return the input buffer even though
104:      * a token has not yet been encountered
105:      *
106:      * @param context ThreadContext
107:      * @return Buffer contents
108:      */
109:     @JRubyMethod
110:     public IRubyObject flush(final ThreadContext context) {
111:         final IRubyObject buffer = input.join(context);
112:         input.clear();
113:         inputSize = 0;
114:         return buffer;
115:     }
